data.title: TJX Companies gains after sailing past comparable sales expectations
data.description:
TJX Companies (TJX) pushed higher in early trading on Wednesday after the retailer reported comparable sales growth of 5% in Q3 to smash the consensus expectation of +3.6%. Total revenue was up 7% year-over-year to $15.1 billion for the quarter that ended on November 1.

The Marmaxx business saw comparable sales rise 6% during Q3, while the HomeGoods business saw a 5% comparable sales increase during the period. Comparable sales were up 8% for TJX Canada and were 3% higher for TJX International (Europe and Australia).

During Q3, gross profit margin was 32.6% vs. 31.6% a year ago, driven by a higher merchandise margin and expense leverage on sales. Selling, general and administrative costs as a percent of sales were 20.1 vs. 19.5% a year ago. EPS was reported at $1.28 vs. $1.22 consensus and $1.14 a year ago.

TJX (TJX) generated $1.5 billion of operating cash flow and ended the quarter with $4.6 billion of cash.

TJX's (TJX) inventory position at the end of the quarter was $9.4 billion, vs. $8.4 billion a year ago. Consolidated inventories on a per-store basis, including distribution centers, but excluding inventory in transit and the company’s e-commerce sites, were up 8% on both a reported and constant currency basis versus last year. The inventory position was noted to reflect the "terrific" buying opportunities TJX (TJX) saw in the third quarter.

CEO Ernie Herrman: "The fourth quarter is off to a strong start, the availability of merchandise continues to be outstanding, and we are excited about the deals we are seeing in the marketplace. With our compelling values and ever-changing, fresh assortments of good, better, and best brands, we are convinced that our stores and e-commerce sites are strongly positioned as gifting destinations for value-conscious shoppers this holiday season."

Looking ahead, guidance for the key holiday quarter was set by TJX (TJX) for consolidated comparable sales to be up 2% to 3% and EPS to land in a range of $1.33 to $1.36 (midpoint $1.345) vs. $1.37 consensus.

Shares of TJX Companies (TJX) were up 2.6% in premarket trading to $149.30 vs. the 52-week range of $112.10 to $148.11.

data.title: Larry Summers resigns from OpenAI's board over release of Epstein emails
data.description:
• Former Treasury Secretary, Harvard President and Director of the National Economic Council Larry Summers has resigned from OpenAI's (OPENAI) board of directors over the release of emails between him and convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.
  • “I am grateful for the opportunity to have served, excited about the potential of the company, and look forward to following their progress,” Summers told CNBC.
  • OpenAI's board of directors issued its own statement, saying it “appreciate[s] his many contributions and the perspective he brought to the Board.”
  • Emails between Summers and Epstein were released publicly as part of more than 20,000 documents that the House Oversight and Government Reform Committee obtained via a subpoena.
  • OpenAI has not yet responded to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

(This is breaking news...)

data.title: Griffon beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Griffon press release (GFF): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.54 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $662.18M (+0.4% Y/Y) beats by $30.71M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA for the fourth quarter totaled $137.9 million compared to the prior year quarter of $137.5 million. Adjusted EBITDA, excluding unallocated amounts (primarily corporate overhead) of $15.9 million in the current quarter and $16.0 million in the prior year quarter, totaled $153.8 million, compared to the prior year quarter of $153.6 million. 

  • 2026 Outlook

    We expect Griffon fiscal year 2026 revenue to be $2.5 billion (consensus of $2.54B) and adjusted EBITDA in a range of $580 million to $600 million, excluding unallocated costs of $58 million. Free cash flow, including capital expenditures of $60 million, is expected to exceed net income, with depreciation of $42 million and amortization of $24 million. Fiscal year 2026 interest expense is expected to be $93 million, and Griffon's normalized tax rate is expected to be 28%.

    From a segment perspective, we anticipate 2026 HBP and CPP revenue to be in line with 2025 and EBITDA margin at HBP to be in excess of 30% and at CPP to be approximately 10%.

data.title: Merck HIV therapy matches Gilead’s Biktarvy in late-stage trial
data.description:
Merck (MRK) announced on Wednesday that its single-tablet antiviral regimen, doravirine and islatravir for adults with HIV, was found to be non-inferior to Gilead’s (GILD) once-daily HIV pill Biktarvy in a Phase 3 trial.

The trial for more than 500 newly diagnosed adults with HIV-1 infection assessed the MRK’s oral once-daily two-drug regimen against Gilead’s (GILD) three-drug combo comprising bictegravir, emtricitabine, and tenofovir alafenamide (Biktarvy).

Citing topline data, the New Jersey-based pharma giant said its MK-8591A-053 Phase 3 trial met the primary efficacy criterion related to the percentage of study participants with less than 50 copies of HIV-1 RNA levels per mL at Week 48.

MK-8591A-053 also reached the primary safety objective with doravirine/islatravir, indicating a comparable tolerability profile to BIC/FTC/TAF, the company added.

Merck (MRK) plans to present the results at a future medical event and share them with health authorities. Its New Drug Application for doravirine/islatravir is currently undergoing review at the U.S. FDA with a target action date of April 28, 2026.

data.title: IonQ names new Chief Business Officer
data.description:
• Quantum computing firm IonQ (IONQ) announced on Wednesday that it has named Scott Millard as its new Chief Business Officer.
  • Shares rose fractionally in premarket trading.
  • Millard joins IonQ from Dell, where he was Senior Vice President, Global AI Sales at Dell Technologies. In his role as Chief Business Officer, Millard will report directly to IonQ Chairman and CEO Niccolo de Masi. He is taking over the position from Rima Alameddine, who is leaving the firm to pursue other opportunities, but will remain in an advisory capacity during a transition period.
  • "The record-breaking financial results we announced earlier this month underscore why IonQ is the industry leader in quantum computing, quantum networking, quantum sensing, and quantum security. Our company’s technical leadership as well as our superior unit economics make IonQ the preferred quantum platform company for customers worldwide,” said de Masi in a statement. “As we look ahead at commercializing all segments of our full-stack quantum solution, we believe that Scott’s 30+ years of sales leadership in technology will play a key role in expanding our lead in the quantum industry. In particular, his deep expertise in AI will be an important complement to IonQ's strength in creating and selling quantum + AI solutions.”

data.title: Okeanis Eco Tankers plans share offering to fund Suezmax tanker newbuildings
data.description:
Okeanis Eco Tankers (ECO) -8.8% pre-market Wednesday after disclosing it is planning an offering of new common shares to raise ~$100 million in gross proceeds to help fund the purchase of two tankers to expand its crude fleet.

Okeanis (ECO) said it has agreed to acquire two 157,000 dwt newbuilding Suezmax vessels, currently under construction at Daehan Shipbuilding in South Korea, from an unrelated third-party seller for $97 million each; both units are scheduled for delivery in January 2026.

The subscription price and total number of the offer shares will be determined by the company's board based on an accelerated bookbuilding process.

The company's current fleet consists of six scrubber-fitted suezmaxe and eight scrubber-fitted VLCC tankers, built during 2016-22.
